title: The Mystery of the Pool Noodle Nibbler
summary: When a pool noodle in the backyard is discovered with mysterious bite marks, Mickey, Minnie, and Moll team up with a young girl and her trusty flashlight to find the culprit. Following a trail of crumbs and using a school bell as a lure, they discover the 'Pool Noodle Nibbler' is actually Roberta the Skunk, who has been eating the foam out of hunger and loneliness. Realizing Roberta is simply misunderstood, the group invites her to join Bert's birthday party, a grand celebration filled with music, skunk friends, and cupcakes, turning a neighborhood mystery into a night of new friendships.
audioFilename: Stars & stories: The Pool Noodle Nibbler.m4a

highlightQuote.text: My mystery was the pool noodle nibbler, the pool noodle nibbler!

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: We can both use our flashlights.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And have a story starty.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, that sounds nice.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: That means we can turn on stars at night and through the day. We can put stars everywhere.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Stars everywhere. I love it. Okay. All right. Oh, the light looks even bigger. In the dark it looks bigger, that's true. Wow, it's making some shadows too.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What else do you notice? Now things that are closer to the light make bigger shadows and if I move it back it makes a smaller shadow. See how that ball is making a round shadow?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: What if I press it back? By putting the ball there. The back... or up... or down...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, that's it. Or around and around.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oscar's watching TV with his flashlight.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh Oscar's watching TV? Oscar Mouse, what are you watching on TV?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: He sees my flashlight. And that's where Mickey, Minnie, and Moll, and Minnie and Mickey and Moll were on an adventure.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay. Let's see. Once upon a time, Mickey and Minnie and Moll decided to go out on an adventure at nighttime.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Do you see the light? Hey, do you see that light? Hey Moll, um, do you see the light that's glowing?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Moll said, 'Oh, it's so dark all around but I do see a light glowing. What is that?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's my flashlight.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Hi Izzy kiddo.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hi, I brought this flashlight because it's nighttime and it's dark outside.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, Mickey and Minnie and I were going to go on a night walk but we don't have a flashlight. Would you come with us? So you can use your light and shine your light on things for us?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Uh-huh. Yeah. My light is already shining too.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Hmm, let's go exploring then. I wonder what we'll find.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Maybe someone that bit my pool noodle.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh my gosh, says Moll. You're right.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Someone did bite my pool noodle yesterday.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And whoever bit that pool noodle, says Moll, must have done it during the nighttime. So this is a good time to go search for them. Now, we'll have to be super sleuths to find the pool noodle biter, won't we?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I have something. Let's look for clues like super sleuth sleuth, think, think, think.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, think, think, think and look at the clues, says Moll. And Mickey says, 'Well, why don't we just walk back to where we saw, you know, the pool noodle crumbs and we could start there.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Pool noodle crumbs. I collected so they won't be there anymore.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, well where were they?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They were in my backyard, but I moved them up, up, up the stairs and then up to the screen where it was hanging and I showed Nana what was my mystery.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: My mystery was the pool noodle nibbler, the pool noodle nibbler.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: The pool noodle nibbler! What a mystery! Hey, I bet the pool noodle nibbler must have come over that wall behind the backyard. So let's climb up it.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But I don't think we should thank him, because he must be scary to mice and moles.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You think the pool noodle nibbler might be scary?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I think so. So maybe let's not force him.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, what if we invite him instead? And Moll said, 'Yeah, we could just get a new pool noodle and put it in the yard and maybe we could ring a bell or sing a song or something to get his attention and then he could come and then we could say hello and then we could see why he's been eating pool noodles and see if he needs something else to eat.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Okay, maybe he could... I can ask him to come to Bert's party. If you... Oh, I left Bert downstairs.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. And Mickey said, 'Wait, why is Bert having a party?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Because he's celebrating the pool noodle nibbler's birthday.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: So I have to get... I wanted to give him one of Bert's cupcakes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, that's very nice of you, said Minnie. You know, I have a green pool noodle right here. Okay, I'll put the pool noodle out in the yard and now we just need to make up some way to attract the pool noodle nibbler's attention. How could we do that?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: How about we get some of my Elmer's glue and glue it down and glue a little tiny bell down. We can borrow one of the teacher's from my new school bell.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A bell! Okay.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Stick! We stick it there. Now we can attract him and get his attention.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, we gotta ring the bell too though. Okay, maybe Izzy, can you... we'll go hide behind the garden and you run out and shake the pool noodle so that the bell will ring.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Run, run, run, run. A fox was running and he rang the bell. Ring, ring, ring. Okay, run back. Run, run, run, run.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, now we have to be very quiet. And Moll says, 'Yeah, now we wait and see who's gonna come.' And so they all waited, watching carefully in the dark and it was quiet for a little while. But then, you know what they heard?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: What?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: They heard some footsteps going crunch, crunch, crunch, crunch, crunch, crunch. Someone was walking into the yard and then they heard... what was that sound?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Who are you?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: The sniffing got louder and then suddenly there was a nibbling sound.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Who is it? Ring, ring, says Fox. I'm still ringing the bell.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh dear, said the voice. Uh, hello, is this your, um, hello, hello pool noodle nibbler. Is this your pool noodle by any chance? It's a delicious one.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes, but I left some pool noodle crumbs and I brought them inside because you chewed them. So I'm gonna get a new pool noodle and Mickey has one right here, but you can't chew pool noodles because they will make you even sicker than that.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh dear, you're so, so, so correct about that. I was very sick the other day after I ate some pool noodle, but I couldn't help it. It was so delicious and this one is also so delicious. If I only had something other than pool noodles to eat.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well, there's cupcakes to eat. Yeah, cupcakes at Bert's party.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Really? But normally nobody gives me a cupcake. I'm just a skunk. Everyone thinks I'm stinky and doesn't let me come to their parties. Boohoo, boohoo.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But, but there's special cupcakes with sprinkles and lots of other skunks are coming to the party.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: They are? That'll be the stinkiest party of all time! It sounds wonderful! When is it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's... it's after this nap and then there's going to be a whole celebration with lots of troops marching into our door. Wow! And cupcakes too, really? And I can have one? Well, thank you so much for the invitation. What is your name? My name is Izzy and this Fox, she's still getting a tooth inside of her. And Oscar came from the NICU yesterday and Baby Jane was just a big girl yesterday but she didn't do well at her new school.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh dear. Why not?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Um, I don't know. She didn't eat snack or take a nap. She just cried and waited for her mom.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I'm sorry to hear that. Do you think she'll do better tomorrow?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't think so. She'll just keep crying and she won't stop.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I hope she can find a way to have a better time than that. I bet she can. She seems like a strong and brave kitten.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Jane always has lots of [Meowing sounds].

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, I'm Roberta the Skunk, also known to some, I suppose, as the pool noodle nibbler. And I look forward to joining you for Bert's party and meeting all these other skunks and eating a cupcake. Thank you for the invitation. And that's...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: This is a scratch cat invitation, you know that. And there's a sign that says, 'Please come to Bert's party' until it's nighttime. There will be lots of people being in the crowd finding their sleeping bag and taking a rest. 'Please come to the party.' With music and other people.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: How very nice. What a lovely invitation. Thank you. Wow. Okay. That's the end of our story. Well, I'm glad we solved the mystery of the pool noodle nibbler.

characters.name: Mickey
characters.description: One of Mistress Mouse’s storied stuffy mice, this famous plush adventurer is a visionary benefactor who manifests fragrant blooms from magic Legos and pilots his Mousecar through lightless depths. Whether arming moles for Kitty’s investigations or securing solutions for Frasgle, he remains fiercely loyal to Minnie, Mole, Izzy, and Hattie. Accompanied by a cheerful infant and shadowed by a friend’s disappearance, his haste to reach the spires of Peacock’s castle has left him without essential provisions, miring him in a sudden, hungry peril.

characters.name: Minnie
characters.description: A wise veteran of briny depths and interstellar frontiers, Mickey’s resourceful mouse companion and confidant to the Princess of Mouseland guides Izzy and Hattie with a glowing lantern and ingenious Mystery Mousketools. Having recently breathed life into a towering guardian of interlocking bricks, this intrepid traveler now points the way toward the mountain’s peak, pausing to investigate the savory aroma of bacon-scented mushrooms while serving as a compassionate friend whose seasoned instincts help decipher Frasgle’s complex roars and sounds.

characters.name: Moll
characters.description: A mole who joins Mickey and Minnie on their night walk.

characters.name: Oscar
characters.description: Hunkered in velvety shadows beside one of Mistress Mouse’s tattered stuffy mice, Lily’s brother illuminates a salvaged television with a scavenged flashlight, contentedly nibbling on oily debris. His focus drifts from the flickering screen to the miniature green car mired in a mud puddle—a casualty of the same reckless speed he employs when racing his yellow school bus. Between these high-stakes heats, he engineers sturdy Lego bridges that arch over the surrounding wreckage, all under the unblinking watch of his glass-eyed, plush sentinel.

characters.name: Bert
characters.description: Stepping from the quiet ink of a library book to join the trek into the deep dark, this classic figure from a shared basement apartment maintains the rhythmic precision and meditative focus of a sleep-story weaver. Formerly the host of grand pigeon galas who folded his roommate Ernie’s vibrant chaos into serene harmony, he now applies a caretaker’s grace to the encroaching shadows. Whether smoothing mud-stained hems into crisp geometries or sorting through the abyss’s chaotic clutter, he treats the exploration of the unknown as a soothing ritual—a singular, exacting discipline of folding the world’s wilder reaches back into a state of restorative order.

characters.name: Roberta
characters.description: A skunk, also known as the pool noodle nibbler, who was eating foam because she was hungry.

characters.name: Fox
characters.description: Behind a ceremonial mask and a playful sticker, this solitary navigator charts both the stars and the thicket, serving as a forest inhabitant who provides directions in exchange for a breakfast of flowers and juice. A steadfast ritualist and fire-extinguisher, they toll the iconic bell for the nibbler while finding more solace in space tacos than the decadent elixirs brewed in their honor. Whether navigating the heavy void or the woodland path, they remain a wise neighbor of cosmic perspective, pointedly advising seekers that the path to clarity begins with a simple bath. Bound to the chime that draws the visitor, they are a figure of quiet contrast—a guide who understands that the weight of destiny is best balanced by the essential purities of the everyday.

characters.name: Baby Jane
characters.description: A kitten who is struggling to adjust to her new school.

characters.name: Nana
characters.description: The dignified mole matriarch and grandmother to Hattie, Pistis, and Isabella, this pillar of watchful wisdom resides at the end of the train line, tackling life’s problems "one at a time." From engineering mouse-scale Lego structures to the meticulous restoration of dollhouses using patient glue and tiny pencils, her steady paws bridge the gap between architectural feats and delicate artistry. Whether discerning potential as a trolley passenger or welcoming weary travelers into her sanctuary, her quiet authority is so legendary that she graces the cover of Hattie’s book in a rare, peaceful slumber. Even in rest, she remains the heart of their world—a steady warmth anchoring every journey’s end, her sharp eyes softening for Izzy’s mushroom-textured crafts.

characters.name: Teacher
characters.description: A figure found lingering in the dim recesses of the toy store, acting as the quiet custodian who provides the group with the loan of a heavy, brass school bell.

places.name: Backyard
places.description: Behind Hattie’s workshop and Uncle Griscko’s house, an eclectic sprawl of junk litters a landscape once home to a lush kelp forest, long since devoured by a mysterious shadow. A towering spaceship now looms over the site’s inaugural breach—a raw, jagged excavation where the earth was first struck and the bitten pool noodle was originally unearthed.

places.name: The Screen
places.description: Where the pool noodle crumbs were displayed to solve the mystery.

places.name: The Garden
places.description: Behind the house, a junk-strewn expanse gives way to the tangled thicket, a strategic blind overlooking the decimated vegetable patch. Once used to ambush the pool noodle nibbler, it now guards the hollowed stalks of a woodchuck’s feast, its floor a messy tapestry of discarded refuse and rabbit droppings.

places.name: New School
places.description: The place where Baby Jane had a difficult day.

places.name: Bert's Party
places.description: A festive celebration featuring music, cupcakes, and sleeping bags.
